summary: Summary: Speaker2 opens the conversation neutrally by recommending Craigslist for job leads. Speaker1 responds with frustration and surprise, skeptical of the suggestion and mentioning they applied for the same theater manager position their friend got. The discussion moves to resumes: Speaker1 voices anger and frustration about the expectation to have a physical resume, while Speaker2 stays neutral, explains the benefits of an outline, and offers help. Tensions rise as Speaker1, experiencing sadness and continued frustration, says they feel discriminated against for not owning a car and explains they choose the bus to save the environment; Speaker2 remains neutral with practical suggestions like carpooling but, after being called “nobody,” expresses surprise and frustration and asks Speaker1 not to take their anger out on them. By the end, despite ongoing frustration, Speaker1 proceeds to describe how they reach out and share qualifications, and Speaker2 continues to offer neutral, practical support.
